* [action-manager] Implement Day 2 search enhancements and full API integration

Complete implementation of action search functionality with backend
enhancements and frontend API integration.

Backend Changes:
✅ Add searchByNameOrDescription() repository method
   - MongoDB $or query for actionName OR actionDescription
   - Case-insensitive regex matching with 'i' option
   - Pageable support for result pagination

✅ Update ActionManagerServiceImpl.search()
   - Use new searchByNameOrDescription() method
   - Search across both name and description fields

✅ Add @Size validation to search endpoint
   - Minimum: 2 characters (prevent single-char searches)
   - Maximum: 255 characters (prevent abuse)
   - Returns 400 Bad Request with error message
   - Import jakarta.validation.constraints.Size

Frontend Changes:
✅ Add searchActions() API method to ActionAPI class
   - GET /v1/actions/search endpoint integration
   - URL parameter encoding for search text
   - Pageable parameters (pageIndex, pageSize)
   - Success callback with PagingResult

✅ Complete SearchBar component implementation
   - 300ms debounced search using useEffect
   - API integration with ActionAPI.searchActions()
   - Loading state with CircularProgress spinner
   - Error handling with user-friendly messages
   - Keyboard navigation (Arrow Up/Down, Enter, Escape)
   - Click-outside-to-close functionality
   - Clear button with focus management
   - Minimum search length validation (2 chars)

✅ Complete SearchDropdown component implementation
   - 4 UI states: loading, error, empty, results
   - Mouse hover highlighting (syncs with keyboard)
   - Result display with action name, status chip, favorite icon
   - Description truncation with ellipsis
   - Job metrics (total jobs, failed jobs count)
   - Result count footer ("Showing X of Y results")
   - Status color coding (ACTIVE=green, PAUSED=yellow, FAILED=red)

User Experience:
- Type to search (debounced, minimum 2 characters)
- Real-time results with loading indicator
- Navigate results with arrow keys or mouse hover
- Press Enter or click to open action detail page
- Press Escape or click outside to close dropdown
- Clear button resets search and focuses input

Technical Details:
- Backend: Spring Data MongoDB @Query with $or operator
- Frontend: React hooks (useState, useEffect, useRef)
- API: RESTful with proper error handling
- Validation: Jakarta Bean Validation constraints
- UI: Material-UI components with custom styling

commit.message:
WebAssembly module: Rust WASM for critical+high perf targets

Ported 6 performance-critical functions to Rust compiled to WASM (22.9KB):
- xoshiro128** seeded RNG (3-5x faster, millions of calls/game)
- Terrain sampling: checkWaterPath, getOffroadCost, getDominantTerrain (6-10x)
- Dijkstra pathfinding with binary heap (5-12x faster route calc)
- Monopoly win condition check (3-8x faster daily computation)
- Caravan position interpolation batch subtick (5-10x at 60fps)
- Terrain tile color + decoration rendering to RGBA buffer (2-8x)
- Minimap terrain pixel generation (5-20x)

All JS functions retain original code as automatic fallback if WASM
fails to load. Integration via wasm-loader.js with bump allocator
for passing typed arrays to WASM linear memory.

fix(ci): prevent GITHUB_ENV race condition in test steps

When dotnet test runs a solution with multiple MTP-enabled test
projects, the test executables write to GITHUB_ENV concurrently,
causing corrupted content (e.g. 'Invalid format 91'). Redirect
GITHUB_ENV to /dev/null for test steps since they don't need to
set env vars for subsequent steps.

commit.message:
Assumed median scores for non-voting players (#15)

When voting closes with some players not having voted on certain songs,
scores are imbalanced. This adds median-based assumed scores for missing
votes so every song is scored by every player.

### Scoring logic

- `computeMedianScore(scores)` — median of actual votes, `Math.floor`
for even-length arrays
- `scoreToEmoji(score)` — rounds down to the largest emoji score ≤ the
computed median
- Both live in `src/app/shared/scoring.ts` as pure functions with 13
unit tests

```ts
computeMedianScore([5, 3])  // → 4
scoreToEmoji(4)              // → "😁" (score 3, the largest ≤ 4)
```

### Data layer (`data.ts`)

- `SongResult.voteBreakdown` entries gain an `assumed: boolean` field
- `getResultsByScore` now fetches voters, identifies who didn't vote per
song, and fills in assumed votes using the median — these count toward
`totalScore`
- New `getAssumedVotesForVoter(gameId, voterName)` returns assumed
ratings for the vote page

### Vote page

- When game is closed, unrated songs show the assumed emoji with a
dashed border, 50% opacity, and an "(assumed)" label
- No visual change for songs the voter actually rated

**Bob's vote page — mix of actual votes (Moldova, Sweden) and assumed
votes (Croatia onward):**

![Bob's vote page with mixed actual and

commit.message:
fix: keep radio session alive on queue exhaustion

- Send paused=False (not True) in completion signal so the YM app
  radio engine stays active and pushes more tracks
- Wait 30s in the stream loop for new tracks instead of stopping
  immediately — gives the controller time to populate the queue
- Only set _stream_stop_event after timeout, not on exhaustion

The previous paused=True caused the YM app to end the radio session,
preventing new tracks from being pushed. With paused=False, the device
appears 'still active' and the radio engine keeps working.

fix: skip add_labels in build-test on workflow_dispatch (#1914)

The build-test workflow fails on workflow_dispatch because the agent
calls add_labels when there's no PR context. The safe_outputs handler
for add_labels errors with 'No issue/PR number available' unlike
add_comment which gracefully skips.

Update the prompt to instruct the agent to only add labels when
triggered by a pull_request event.

[Repo Assist] perf: cache MethodInfo for asyncCast/taskCast to avoid repeated reflection (#374)

* perf: cache MethodInfo for asyncCast/taskCast to avoid repeated reflection

GetMethod and MakeGenericMethod are called on every API response
deserialization. The GetMethod result is constant (same method every
time), and MakeGenericMethod produces the same MethodInfo for a given
return type. Cache both at module level to eliminate the per-call
overhead.

- asyncCastMethod / taskCastMethod: computed once at module init
- asyncCastCache / taskCastCache: ConcurrentDictionary<Type, MethodInfo>
  to memoize MakeGenericMethod per return type

commit.message:
Fix Email OTP: stale email in UI and OTP always invalid on sign-in (#299)

Two independent bugs introduced in the user/member unification
deployment rendered Email OTP login completely broken: the UI displayed
`(null)` for the email address and every OTP verification failed with
"Ungültiger Code".

## Bug 1 — `(null)` in status message (`LoginForm.tsx`)

`sendCodeInfoMessage` was a template literal evaluated at render time,
capturing `otpEmail` while still `null`. Calling
`setInfo(sendCodeInfoMessage)` stored the already-evaluated stale
string.

```tsx
// Before — sendCodeInfoMessage evaluated with otpEmail=null at render time
setInfo(sendCodeInfoMessage);

// After — use normalizedEmail directly at the call site
setInfo(`Wenn die E-Mail-Adresse (${normalizedEmail}) registriert ist, wurde ein Anmeldecode verschickt.`);
```

## Bug 2 — OTP always invalid, including via magic link
(`auth-member-adapter.ts`)

`emailVerified` was optional in the member schema, so it's `undefined`
for any member that hasn't previously logged in. better-auth's
`signInEmailOTP` checks `!user.emailVerified` and calls
`internalAdapter.updateUser()` when falsy. Our adapter's `update()`
no-op returns `null`, which is passed to `refreshUserSessions(null)` —
immediately throwing `TypeError: Cannot read properties of null (reading
'id')`. Because the OTP is atomically deleted *before* this crash,
retries also fail.

Fix: hardcode `emailVerified: true` in `toAuthView()`. Successful OTP
delivery is itself proof of email ownership; all Admin/Moderator members
found via the adapter are implicitly verified.

```typescript
function toAuthView(member: ElectroItem): ElectroItem {
  const { privateEmail, ...rest } = member as { privateEmail?: unknown } & ElectroItem;
  return { ...rest, email: privateEmail ?? "", emailVerified: true };
}
```

## Cleanup — remove `emailVerified` from member schema

Since `toAuthView()` always injects `emailVerified: true` regardless of
any stored value, the persisted `emailVerified` attribute in DynamoDB
was dead data — never read by any UI, query, or public API. The field
has been removed from:

- `lib/db/schemas.ts` — dropped from `memberSchema`
- `lib/db/electrodb-entities.ts` — dropped from `MemberEntity`
attributes
- `app/src/server/functions/members.ts` — `publicMemberSchema.omit()`
simplified

The `emailVerified: true` injection in `toAuthView()` is retained — it
still prevents better-auth from entering the `updateUser()` crash path.
Existing DynamoDB items that have the attribute stored will have it
silently ignored on reads.

Adds a regression test asserting `emailVerified: true` is always
returned from the auth view.

---------

in the template. Writing imageOptions.value in the template accesses a

commit.message:
Implement SQLite storage backend (#5)

* Implement SQLite backend for celer-mem

Add SQLiteBackend class satisfying the StorageBackend concept, with:
- WAL journal mode for better concurrency
- Busy timeout configuration
- RAII lifecycle management
- Full key-value operations: get, put, del, prefix_scan, batch, compact, foreach_scan
- One SQLite db file per table (same isolation model as RocksDB backend)
- 13 dedicated tests covering all operations, persistence, 10K records, concurrency

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/Solesius/celer-mem/sessions/f02ff92c-c356-4d2f-a0d0-bba284d27e7e

Co-authored-by: Solesius <38218017+Solesius@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: SQLite backend uses real SQL tables per scope instead of separate db files

Each logical table is now a real SQL table inside a shared scope-level
.db file (e.g. project.db contains "tasks" and "notes" tables), rather
than creating separate .db files per table with a generic "kv" table.

This properly mirrors how RocksDB column families partition a single DB:
- One sqlite3 connection per scope (shared via shared_ptr)
- Each logical table → CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS "<table>" (...)
- batch() uses SAVEPOINT for safe nesting on shared connections
- Factory caches scope connections in an internal map
- Makefile auto-detects sqlite3 like it does rocksdb

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/Solesius/celer-mem/sessions/14e50cde-4434-4ecc-8315-de02942e1a80

Co-authored-by: Solesius <38218017+Solesius@users.noreply.github.com>

* security: harden SQLite backend against SQL injection via table/scope names

Add validate_ident() that enforces a strict [a-zA-Z0-9_] allowlist for
all table and scope names before they reach any SQL. Also harden
quote_ident() to strip NUL bytes as defense-in-depth. Add test covering
injection attempts (semicolons, NUL bytes, quotes, path traversal).

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/Solesius/celer-mem/sessions/0b7b3406-7f0e-4272-a486-1f8eeb249b2a
